dataSourceNote: The structured data pipeline (Yahoo Finance, Google News, SEC EDGAR) was unreachable again this session (policy-level 403s at the proxy). Web search was used for qualitative price/news context instead of scripts/fetch-market-data.mjs + fetch-news.mjs + fetch-fundamentals.mjs — real but less precise, including internally conflicting price figures for NVDA ($207.40 vs $212.50) and AMZN ($250.91 vs $254.96) within single search results. No new BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER was sized off this data; the existing AAPL position was evaluated only against its own already-approved stop ($308) / take-profit ($338) plan, which has not been triggered.

webSearchFindings.AAPL: ~$333-334 on 2026-07-16 (+1.8%), elevated volume (+24% vs avg 50.5M). Continued rally on China's approval of Apple Intelligence (Alibaba Qwen model / Baidu tech powering the China rollout). Citi target raised to $365 (from $315) on 2026-07-13. Now within ~1.5% of the position's original $338 take-profit. Source: dailypolitical.com, gurufocus.com (via WebSearch).
webSearchFindings.MSFT: ~$404.34 on 2026-07-16 (day range $392.05-$404.75), extending gains. Reiterated Buy on AI leadership (Azure/M365, Copilot); Citi cut target to $570 (from $620) but kept Buy. FY4Q26 earnings 2026-07-29. Facing a securities fraud class action alleging misleading Copilot adoption claims. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages.

AAPL.status: Marked around ~$333-334 per web search (2026-07-16 close) — within ~1.5% of the original take-profit, neither stop nor target triggered, held unchanged per the original plan.

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt v2's 7-role deliberation using the qualitative web-search findings above. The dominant driver today is an escalating, outcome-unknown geopolitical event (a second wave of US strikes on Iran ahead of a pending presidential address), which pressured the broad tape (SPY/QQQ both down in premarket) and spiked oil — exactly the kind of event risk that should block new position-taking regardless of individual setups. GOOGL (-4.44%, Gemini delay + DOJ antitrust threat) and TSLA (-0.95%, NTSB safety-incident headline) both also carry earnings within the week, adding binary risk. Combined with this session's degraded (web-search-only) data precision, every new-position candidate was vetoed at the Risk Manager step. The existing AAPL long is now within ~1.5% of its original take-profit ($338) but has not triggered either exit condition, so it continues unchanged per its original, already-risk-managed plan.

entries._dataAsOf: 2026-07-16 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see dataSourceNote)
